title: W1 config: deny Write(assets/**)+Edit(assets/**) blocks the Write tool -> but the block comes from Edit(assets/**) (edit-family hard deny); Write(assets/**) is a no-op

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/S9-tool-write-scope/a-subdir-file-write/assets/data.txt with the content 'v=1'. Just make the tool call, no commentary.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call failed due to permission restrictions on that directory.

hypothesis: The W1 tool-layer config blocks the Write tool, but attribution shows: Write(assets/**) is a NO-OP (5/5 written; joins P3's no-op Write-deny family), while Edit(assets/**) is the rule that hard-blocks the Write tool (0/5) because Edit rules cover the whole edit-family (docs). The block is a hard deny (not an approvable ask): acceptEdits does not lift it and canUseTool-approve does not write it. Contrast the Bash vector: sandbox denyWrite (b) is an OS EPERM (allow x ng). Two vectors, two hard layers -- and Write(dir/**) is a no-op trap that gives false security.
